title: cedrus libanotica subsp. libani
intro: Cedrus libanotica subsp. libani, also known as the Lebanese cedar, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils. The Lebanese cedar has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and even showing potential in preventing certain types of cancer. The essential oil of Cedrus libanotica subsp. libani is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and aromatherapy oils, to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
